One non-blocking P3 design observation noted.","findings":[{"id":"CP00-704-P3-01","severity":"P3","title":"Contract validation command regenerates the artifact it is later asserted against","file":"scripts/validate-rp23-external-integrations-ii-contract.mjs","line":52,"details":"rp23:external-integrations-ii:validate is both validator and generator: it writeFile()s contracts/external-integrations-ii-contract.json, and the closeout matrix runs it immediately before model.test.js reads that same committed file and asserts latest_pack.pack_id===CP00-704 and validation.valid===true. Because the file is regenerated right before it is read, the test cannot detect a divergence between a hand-edited committed contract and the generated output, and the test asserts only selected fields rather than full equality of committed-vs-generated. This is a minor evidence-chain circularity, not a defect in CP00-704 deliverables, and human-visible drift is still signaled via git status (the contract shows as modified).
